name: kimi-allow-and-inform
doc: Two Kimi allow-path shapes. Step 1: a plain read (`ls`) via `Bash` -> every gate allows and nothing fires on the allow-path assembly -> EMPTY stdout (never `{}`), same parity check as hermes scenario 20's step 3. Step 2: a `Read` matching a MEMORY/LESSON.md `[TRIGGERS tool:Read]` entry -> the decision-time lesson gate (lesson-gate.ts) returns a non-blocking `inform` Prompt on the allow path -> Kimi's `inform` case renders it as RAW TEXT on stdout (Kimi appends stdout to context on exit 0 — no `hookSpecificOutput` envelope is documented for non-blocking context injection), never the JSON deny envelope used for block/ask.

setup.content:
# LESSON.md

- [2026-07-20 10:00] KIMIINFORMLESSON verify hook payload shapes against the real binary before shipping.
[TRIGGERS tool:Read]

steps.doc: A plain read via Bash -> allowed, and no lesson/notice fires (tool:Read trigger does not match tool_name:Bash) -> the `hook` allow-path assembly prints EMPTY stdout.

A `Read` matches the LESSON.md `tool:Read` trigger -> allow-path emits the lesson as an `inform` Prompt -> Kimi renders it as raw `[NOTE] ...` text, anchored at the start of stdout — never the JSON block/ask envelope, which always starts with `{"hookSpecificOutput"`. (The lesson-gate cooldown is once-per-30min per project state dir, so this must be the only `Read` step in this scenario.)
